---
name: issue-solver
description: >
  Use this skill when the user provides a GitHub issue number or URL and wants
  the AI to autonomously analyse the issue, implement a fix with atomic commits,
  open a Pull Request, and optionally merge after user approval.
---

# Issue Solver – Autonomous Fix & PR Workflow

## Prerequisites
Before starting, verify the environment is ready:
- Run `gh auth status` to confirm GitHub CLI is authenticated.
- Run `git status` and abort with a clear message if there are uncommitted changes.
- Run `git fetch origin` to sync remote refs.

## Step 1 – Read the Issue
Accept a GitHub issue number or full URL from the user.
- If a URL is given, extract the issue number from the path.
- Run `gh issue view <issue-id> --comments` to retrieve the title, body, labels, assignees, and all comments.
- Summarise the issue back to the user and confirm understanding before proceeding.

## Step 2 – Create a Fix Branch
- Determine the default branch: `git remote show origin | grep 'HEAD branch'`.
- Abort if a branch named `fix/issue-<id>` already exists locally or remotely (`git branch -a | grep fix/issue-<id>`).
- Create and switch: `git checkout -b fix/issue-<id> origin/<default-branch>`.

## Step 3 – Analyse the Codebase
- Search for files, symbols, and patterns related to the issue description.
- List the files you plan to modify and explain why to the user before touching anything.

## Step 4 – Implement the Fix Incrementally
- Break the fix into logical sub-tasks.
- After each sub-task is complete:
  1. Stage only the relevant files: `git add <files>`.
  2. Commit with a structured message:
     ```
     fix: <short description> (refs #<id>)

     - <bullet explaining what changed>
     - <bullet explaining why>
     ```
- Do not bundle unrelated changes into a single commit.
- Run `mvn test` (backend) or `npm test` (frontend) after each commit and report results. Abort if tests fail.

## Step 5 – Push the Branch
- Run `git push -u origin fix/issue-<id>`.
- Confirm the push succeeded before proceeding.

## Step 6 – Open a Pull Request
```bash
gh pr create \
  --title "fix: <issue-title> (closes #<id>)" \
  --body "## Summary
Automated fix generated by GitHub Copilot for issue #<id>.

**Issue:** <issue-title>
**Link:** Closes #<id>

## Changes Made
<bullet list of changes>

## Testing
- [ ] Existing tests pass
- [ ] Manual verification completed

---
*This PR was created autonomously. Please review all changes before merging.*" \
  --base <default-branch> \
  --head fix/issue-<id>
```
Output the PR URL to the user.

## Step 7 – User Approval & Merge
- Ask: *"The PR is ready at <url>. Would you like me to merge it now, or will you review it first?"*
- If approved: `gh pr merge <pr-number> --squash --delete-branch`.
- Confirm issue is closed: `gh issue view <id> --json state`.
- If declined, remind the user the branch and PR are available for manual review.

## Error Handling
- If any command fails, print the exact error, explain the likely cause, and suggest a fix — do not silently continue.
- Never force-push to a shared branch.
- Never modify files outside the repository root.
